duplicates.description: Groups of open findings that describe the same underlying vulnerability.

canonical_id.description: Database id of the finding that should remain active.

duplicate_ids.description: Database ids of open findings to mark duplicate.

reason.description: Why these findings are the same underlying vulnerability.

subsumed.description: Findings that are only reachable through another finding, such that any correct fix for the parent closes them too. Distinct from duplicates: the bugs are different, but the child does not need its own disclosure.

parent_id.description: Database id of the finding whose fix closes the subsumed ones.

subsumed_ids.description: Database ids of open findings to mark subsumed by the parent.

reason.description: Why the parent's fix necessarily closes these.

chains.description: Groups of two or more findings that combine into a higher-severity attack than any of them alone. All members stay open; disclose reports them together.

finding_ids.description: Database ids of open findings that chain together. Order is the exploit order when it matters.

reason.description: How the chain works and why the combined severity is higher.
